codeReview.summary: The fix directly resolves the saved-view regression. applyView() now restores debouncedSearch alongside searchQuery, matching the store's URL hydration path, and the new store regression test proves saved search filters take effect immediately after loading a view.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill should explicitly allow pure logic/store fixes with direct automated coverage to satisfy verification without requiring agent-browser runs when there is no meaningful user-surface interaction to exercise.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The fix-saved-view-search-restore handoff shows a one-line store correction in src/stores/filterStore.js plus a precise regression test in test/stores.test.js; the worker's skillFeedback notes that browser automation was unnecessary because the bug lived entirely in store state restoration.

summary: Pass. Saved views now immediately reapply saved search filters because applyView restores both searchQuery and debouncedSearch.
